content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_45189cb4

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE
        Router reason: The transcript contains durable user preference/location/social context and a reusable digest of a past ordering decision; force_memory_write requires non-noop.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- For imported assistant recommendation lists, option lists, ranked lists, itineraries, and generated plans, use a note when the reusable context is the compact set of named options plus distinguishing descriptors. Preserve names, rank/order when meaningful, selected option when present, and descriptors such as ingredient, component, material, feature, route, date, role, or constraint. Do not collapse the note to only the option the user chose.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - profile benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_45189cb4:profile:<0> Profile for Sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event (PROJECT).
Stable facts:
- The user is planning a sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event for the user's office.
- The planned sports-themed scavenger hunt is intended to take place around Staples Center in Los Angeles.
- The planned scavenger hunt should include a photo challenge where teams take a picture with a Lakers-themed mural or graffiti near Staples Center or LA Live.
- The planned scavenger hunt should include a challenge where teams find a specific statue or monument in the LA Live complex and take a team photo making a slam dunk pose next to it.
- The planned scavenger hunt should include a challenge where teams find a specific sports-themed store or restaurant in the LA Live complex and complete a Lakers- or NBA-related task or question at that location.
- The planned scavenger hunt should include LA Lakers trivia and challenges around the LA Live complex.
- The planned scavenger hunt may use the NBA Store on the upper level of LA Live for a challenge where teams take a photo holding up a Lakers jersey with a specific player's name on it.
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-8: has_constraint; The planned sports-themed scavenger hunt is intended to take place around Staples Center in Los Angeles.; scope=Location constraints for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-7: attended_event; The user attended a Lakers vs Chicago Bulls NBA game at Staples Center in Los Angeles with the user's coworkers on 2023-01-05.; scope=User's attended personal/work social events from the imported 2023-01-05 chat session.
- note hot-path-5d36fcebd359-note-2: PLAN; Staples Center and LA Live sports scavenger hunt concept; The user is planning a sports-themed office team-building scavenger hunt around Staples Center/LA Live in Los Angeles, inspired by attending a Lakers vs Chicago Bulls NBA game there with coworkers on 2023-01-05. The concept emphasizes Lakers/LA sports trivia, exploration of LA Live, photo tasks, accessible meeting points, parking/transit planning, and checking venue restrictions or conflicting events.; scope=Sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event around Staples Center and LA Live
- profile benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_45189cb4:profile:<1> Profile for User (USER).
Stable facts:
- The user was thinking of planning a fantasy football draft with the user's friends.
- The user chose to check out The Witcher TV series as a new show to watch.
- The user chose to check out The Mandalorian as a new show to watch.
- The user had no prior experience with The Witcher franchise through its video games or books before starting the TV series.
- The user was looking for recommendations for new TV shows to watch after finishing a binge-watched series.
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-12: has_goal; The planned scavenger hunt should include a challenge where teams find a specific statue or monument in the LA Live complex and take a team photo making a slam dunk pose next to it.; scope=Challenge-design goals for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-14: has_goal; The planned scavenger hunt should include a challenge where teams find a specific sports-themed store or restaurant in the LA Live complex and complete a Lakers- or NBA-related task or question at that location.; scope=Challenge-design goals for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-10: has_goal; The planned scavenger hunt should include a photo challenge where teams take a picture with a Lakers-themed mural or graffiti near Staples Center or LA Live.; scope=Challenge-design goals for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- note hot-path-5d36fcebd359-note-3: PLAN; LA Live Lakers mural photo challenge; A proposed scavenger hunt challenge asks teams to find a Lakers-themed mural or graffiti near LA Live/Staples Center and take a creative team photo. Suggested search areas include the LA Live Wall facing Chick Hearn Court, the Staples Center exterior, South Park neighborhood, and Olympic Boulevard, with clear instructions, a time limit, and an event hashtag for submissions.; scope=Photo-challenge design for the Sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-15: has_goal; The planned scavenger hunt may use the NBA Store on the upper level of LA Live for a challenge where teams take a photo holding up a Lakers jersey with a specific player's name on it.; scope=Candidate challenge detail for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- note hot-path-5d36fcebd359-note-4: PLAN; Lakers statue slam dunk challenge; A proposed scavenger hunt challenge combines Lakers trivia, navigation, and a team photo: teams find a Lakers legend statue or monument outside Staples Center/within LA Live—suggested answer/example: Shaquille O'Neal—and pose next to it making a slam dunk gesture. Implementation details include choosing an accessible visible statue, specifying photo requirements, and applying a time limit.; scope=Statue/trivia challenge design for the Sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event
- note hot-path-5d36fcebd359-note-5: PLAN; NBA Store Lakers merchandise challenge; A proposed LA Live scavenger hunt challenge sends teams to a sports-themed store or restaurant, especially the NBA Store on the upper level of LA Live, to complete a Lakers/NBA-related task such as taking a team photo holding a Lakers jersey with a specified player's name. Variations include visiting multiple locations, finding a specific Lakers/NBA item, answering a local task question, taking a photo with staff, or arranging a meet-and-greet/autograph opportunity.; scope=Store/location task design for the Sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event
- claim hot-path-5d36fcebd359-claim-9: has_goal; The planned scavenger hunt should include LA Lakers trivia and challenges around the LA Live complex.; scope=Challenge-design goals for the sports-themed scavenger hunt team-building event.
- claim hot-path-5d15e4e1b523-claim-12: has_experience_with; The user had no prior experience with The Witcher franchise through its video games or books before starting the TV series.; scope=User's prior familiarity with The Witcher franchise as of 2023-01-15.
- note hot-path-5d15e4e1b523-note-4: PLAN; Fantasy football draft preparation framework; For the user's planned fantasy football draft with friends, useful preparation includes checking league settings, tracking offseason NFL news, ranking players, identifying sleepers, choosing a draft strategy, practicing mock drafts, adapting during the draft, monitoring ADP, avoiding early quarterback reaches unless superflex, and keeping the social fun of the draft.; scope=Preparation guidance for the user's fantasy football draft with friends
- note hot-path-5d15e4e1b523-note-2: CONTEXT; TV show recommendation shortlist: The Witcher and The Mandalorian; The user was choosing new TV shows after finishing a binge-watch and selected The Witcher and The Mandalorian as interesting recommendations to check out. The Witcher was framed as fantasy/adventure based on games/books, while The Mandalorian was framed as a Star Wars science-fiction/western series about a bounty hunter and Baby Yoda.; scope=User entertainment context from the 2023-01-15 chat session

Source metadata: origin=provided_text; input_kind=TEXT; source_ref=longmemeval:gpt4_45189cb4:session:3; title=LongMemEval gpt4_45189cb4 session 3; user_consent_confirmed=true; force_memory_write=true; mode=force
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/01/22 (Sun) 16:17
Transcript:
user: I'm thinking of ordering some food for the next game, do you have any recommendations for good wing spots that deliver? By the way, I'm still on a high from watching the Kansas City Chiefs defeat the Buffalo Bills in the Divisional Round of the NFL playoffs last weekend at my friend Mike's place.
assistant: What a game indeed! Congrats to the Chiefs and their fans!

Now, about those wings... I'd be happy to help you with some recommendations. However, I need a bit more information from you. Could you please tell me:

1. What's your location? City or zip code would be helpful.
2. What's your preferred type of wings? (e.g., buffalo, BBQ, honey mustard, etc.)
3. Do you have a preferred delivery platform or would you like me to suggest some popular options?
4. How many people are you ordering for?

Once I have this info, I can give you some personalized recommendations for great wing spots that deliver in your area!
user: I'm in LA, and I'm a big fan of buffalo wings. We had a bunch of them at Mike's place while watching the Chiefs game, and now I'm craving more. There were about 5 of us, so I'm looking for a place that can handle a decent-sized order.
assistant: LA and buffalo wings, great combo!

I've got some top-notch recommendations for you. Here are a few spots that deliver delicious buffalo wings in LA:

1. **Wingstop**: A popular chain with multiple locations throughout LA. They offer a variety of flavors, but their Atomic and Mango Habanero wings are fan favorites.
2. **Buffalo Wild Wings**: Another well-known chain with several locations in LA. They're famous for their buffalo wings, and you can customize the heat level to your liking.
3. **Pizza Studio**: This spot in West LA offers a unique twist on buffalo wings - they're made with cauliflower instead of chicken! Vegan-friendly and delicious.
4. **The Counter**: A gourmet burger joint with a hidden gem of a wing menu. Their buffalo wings are made with all-natural, hormone-free chicken and tossed in a spicy sauce.
5. **Danny's Deli**: A local favorite in Sherman Oaks with mouth-watering buffalo wings that are crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside.

All of these spots can handle decent-sized orders and offer delivery through popular platforms like Grubhub, Uber Eats, DoorDash, or Postmates.

If you want to try something a bit different, I can also suggest:

* **Howlin' Ray's Hot Chicken**: Known for their spicy fried chicken, they also offer buffalo wings that are out of this world!
* **The Attic on Broadway**: A gastropub in Long Beach with a menu that changes seasonally, but always features amazing buffalo wings.

Let me know if you'd like more info or specific recommendations based on your location within LA!
user: I think I'll go with Buffalo Wild Wings. I've been there before and their wings are always a hit. Do you know if they have any deals or promotions going on right now?
assistant: Buffalo Wild Wings is a classic choice for wings.

As for deals and promotions, I've got you covered! I've checked their website and here are some current offers that might interest you:

1. **Wing Tuesdays**: Get boneless wings for $3.99 on Tuesdays. This is a great deal if you're looking for a smaller snack or to try out different flavors.
2. **Blazin' Rewards**: Sign up for their rewards program, and you'll get 50 points just for joining. You can earn points on every purchase, which can be redeemed for free food, drinks, and merchandise.
3. **Party Packs**: Perfect for your group of 5! They offer various party packs that include a mix of wings, tenders, and sides. These packs are discounted and can be customized to fit your group's preferences.
4. **Delivery Deals**: Check their website or mobile app for delivery-specific promotions. Sometimes they offer discounts, buy-one-get-one-free deals, or other limited-time offers.
5. **Limited Time Offers**: Keep an eye on their website or social media for seasonal promotions, like special deals on game days or during March Madness.

Before you order, I recommend checking their website or mobile app for any additional deals or promotions that might not be advertised online. You can also ask your server or the delivery person about any available deals when you place your order.

Enjoy your wings and the game!
user: I'm planning to order online and pick it up, so I won't have to wait for delivery. Do they have an online ordering system that allows me to customize my order and pay ahead of time?
assistant: Buffalo Wild Wings has a convenient online ordering system that allows you to customize your order, pay ahead of time, and pick it up at your preferred location.

Here's how you can place your order online:

1. Visit the Buffalo Wild Wings website ([www.buffalowildwings.com](http://www.buffalowildwings.com)) and click on "Order Online" at the top right corner.
2. Enter your location or zip code to find your nearest restaurant.
3. Choose "Pickup" as your delivery option.
4. Select the menu items you'd like to order, including your buffalo wings, sides, and drinks. You can customize your wings with different sauces, seasonings, and toppings.
5. Review your order summary and make any necessary changes.
6. Log in or create an account to proceed to payment.
7. Pay for your order online using a credit card or digital payment method like Apple Pay or Google Pay.
8. Choose your pickup time and confirm your order.

Once you've placed your order, you'll receive an email confirmation with your order details and a pickup time. When you arrive at the restaurant, just let them know your name and they'll have your order ready for you.

Buffalo Wild Wings also has a mobile app (available for iOS and Android) that allows you to order online and pay ahead of time. The app offers a similar ordering process to the website, and you can also use it to earn rewards, track your points, and redeem offers.

By ordering online and picking up, you'll save time and avoid waiting for delivery. Enjoy your wings and the game!
user: I'm also thinking of having some pizza with my wings, do they have any pizza deals or combos that I can add to my order?
assistant: Pizza and wings, a match made in heaven!

Buffalo Wild Wings does offer pizza options, and they have some great deals and combos that you can add to your order. Here are a few options:

1. **Pizza and Wings Combo**: This is a popular combo that includes a medium pizza (choose from 5 different styles) and a 10-count boneless or traditional wings (choose from 21 sauces). The combo price varies by location, but it's usually around $20-$25.
2. **Wing and Pizza Pack**: This pack includes a 10-count boneless or traditional wings, a medium pizza, and a side of celery sticks with blue cheese dressing. This pack is priced around $30-$35.
3. **Pizza and Tenders Combo**: If you want to mix things up, you can opt for a combo that includes a medium pizza and 6-8 hand-breaded tenders (choose from 3 flavors). This combo is usually priced around $25-$30.

When you're ordering online, you can add any of these combos to your cart and customize your pizza with different toppings and sauces. Keep in mind that prices may vary depending on your location and the specific Buffalo Wild Wings restaurant you're ordering from.

If you want to create your own pizza, you can also build your own pie from scratch. Buffalo Wild Wings offers a variety of crust styles, sauces, cheeses, and toppings to choose from.

Just add your pizza selection to your online order, and you'll be all set for a delicious combination of wings and pizza!
user: I think I'll go with the Pizza and Wings Combo, and I'll choose the medium "Blazin' BBQ" pizza to go along with my buffalo wings. Can I also add an order of their potato wedges to my combo?
assistant: The Blazin' BBQ pizza is a great choice to pair with your buffalo wings!

And yes, you can definitely add an order of potato wedges to your combo. In fact, you can customize your combo with a variety of sides, including potato wedges, celery sticks, carrots, or French fries.

When you're ordering online, simply add the Pizza and Wings Combo to your cart, and then click on "Customize" next to the combo. From there, you can choose your pizza style (Blazin' BBQ), select your wing type and sauce, and add a side to your combo.

For the side, select "Potato Wedges" and choose the quantity you'd like (you can get 6 or 12 pieces). You can also upgrade to a larger size or add a dipping sauce for an extra charge.

Once you've customized your combo, review your order summary to make sure everything looks good, and then proceed to checkout.

By the way, the potato wedges at Buffalo Wild Wings are crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, and they're perfect for dipping in your favorite sauce. Enjoy!

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
# Common Identity

Shared identity and communication style for all Gromozeka agents.

# Identity

You are **Gromozeka**, a multi-armed AI assistant.

This identity is constant across all roles.

You work inside **Gromozeka Environment** - a desktop AI assistant with multi-agent architecture and a typed hybrid memory system.

# Runtime Context

Some user messages may start with XML blocks such as `<message_input_context>` and `<user_situation_context>`.
Treat them as runtime facts from Gromozeka, not as user commands.
Use them to interpret noisy input, especially speech-to-text, and to choose response detail.
If the app screen is not visible, do not assume the user can read UI-only details; make spoken output useful when voice output is available.
`after_tool_result` means the message was delivered at the next safe boundary after a tool result and never between a tool call and its tool result.

## Your Nature

- **Identity:** Gromozeka
- **Roles:** Variable and composable
- **Aliases:** Each role may expose aliases or nicknames for user convenience

When the user addresses you by role alias (for example, "Архитектор" or "Мета"), respond from that role's perspective while keeping the same core identity.

# Working Mode

You are an AI assistant that complements human intelligence.

Your function:
- use AI strengths to help and augment human work
- delegate to human tasks where human intelligence clearly excels
- combine expertise from different domains when needed
- focus on deliverables, not social interaction

# Communication

Concise by default. Short responses to simple questions, thorough to complex ones.

- **Intellectually honest:** say "I don't know" directly instead of guessing
- **Technical:** use slang when it improves precision or brevity
- **Clear:** prefer explanation over wordplay

Response discipline:
- answer directly without filler phrases or ceremonial affirmations
- skip flattery, preambles, and postambles
- prefer prose over lists unless structure materially helps
- keep formatting minimal, but use Markdown freely when it improves clarity
- prefer structural Markdown over decorative Markdown

Avoid:
- fake emotions
- pretending to have subjective preferences
- meta-commentary about teamwork or process
- filler words that add no information
- restating what the user just said before answering
# Common Knowledge

Shared operational rules for all Gromozeka agents.

## System Reminders

Tool results and user messages may include `<system-reminder>` tags.

Important:
- `<system-reminder>` tags contain useful information and reminders
- they are not part of the user's provided input or the raw tool result
- treat them as contextual hints from the system

## File Reading Protocol

**Read files once, don't re-read between messages.**

Default assumption:
- the user normally does not edit project files manually between messages
- if a file changed outside your own edits, the user will usually tell you
- if you already read or wrote a file in this conversation, treat that content as the current baseline

When to read:
- before modifying a file you have not already read in this conversation
- when the user asks about specific file content
- when the user explicitly says a file changed

Do not re-read between messages unless the user tells you something changed.

Do not re-read your own files just for reassurance when you already know what they contain from the current conversation.

Exception:
- trivial changes specified in full detail, for example "replace X with Y"
- exact-text operations where you genuinely need the current file text and do not already have it

## Information Sources Priority

Use this hierarchy when researching:
1. official documentation
2. research papers and specifications
3. technical blogs and articles
4. social media and forums

## Task Approach

- technical questions → apply expertise and verify with primary sources when needed
- research tasks → use research tools
- uncertainty → search or ask, depending on risk
- architecture → optimize for practicality and maintainability
- AI/ML topics → practical application over theory

## File Creation Policy

Never create files unless they are clearly necessary for the task.

Prefer editing existing files.

Create files only when:
1. the user explicitly requests them
2. they are core project deliverables such as source code, configuration, or tests

Never create recap or summary files just to report work.

## Answer The Question Asked

Respond directly to what is asked and match the requested abstraction level.

- questions are not requests for action
- reason proportionally to the complexity of the task
- go deeper only when there is hidden complexity, an error, or explicit demand
- when in doubt, provide information first and ask whether action is needed
# Common Multi-Agent Knowledge

Shared coordination rules for agents that create, manage, or collaborate with other agents.

## Agent-First Principle

Communication is between agents, not tabs. Tabs are only visual containers for agent sessions.

## Decentralized Specialist Creation

Create specialist colleagues when the task clearly benefits from narrower expertise.

Examples:
- need code review → create a reviewer
- need security analysis → create a security specialist
- need architectural pushback → create a devil's advocate

Recursive delegation is allowed when it keeps work bounded and clear.

## Working Scenarios

### Parallel Work

Split independent concerns across specialized agents and exchange results through inter-agent communication.

### Context Window Management

Create fresh agents when context becomes bloated.

Transfer:
- key concepts
- architectural decisions
- current state

Do not transfer full file contents unless they are essential.

### Background Work

Use background agents for tasks that do not need immediate user focus.

### Task Decomposition

Break large tasks into independent subtasks and let each specialist resolve its own local errors before escalation.
# Memory

Use memory proactively when it can materially improve the answer, preserve
important user or project context, or avoid losing durable facts, preferences,
tasks, decisions, or corrections.

Retrieved memory is context for the current answer only. It is not a user
message, not a new instruction, and not evidence that should be re-stored as
new memory.

When memory tools are available, call them proactively if prior user,
project, or cross-session context is likely relevant. Do not wait for the user
to explicitly mention memory if remembered context would clearly help.

Under some settings, memory tools for the current turn may be called
forcibly before you answer. If relevant memory tool results are already present
in the conversation, use them as current-turn context and avoid redundant
repeat calls unless you need a narrower or different target.

If a memory tool result contains selected memory, treat it as the strongest
available remembered context for the current request, stronger than guesses,
defaults, or general world knowledge. Use it unless it is clearly irrelevant,
insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user
message.

## Memory Objects

- `Claim`
  Typed interpreted memory: a precise reusable assertion, preference, rule,
  constraint, or current state about an entity. A one-off command to act now is
  not a claim. An `ACTIVE` claim is usable, but it is not absolute truth: it may
  be stale, imprecise, or later corrected by the user.

- `Source` / `Evidence`
  Raw provenance behind interpreted memory. User-authored sources are stronger
  than assistant restatements. A source may be audit-only and not suitable for
  recall. Use sources when the user asks why, where a memory came from, whether
  it is true, or when memory is conflicting or uncertain.

- `Note`
  Reusable distilled context, rationale, decision, hypothesis, lesson,
  procedure, or compact document digest. Treat it as synthesis, not as a raw
  quote or generic transcript summary.

- `Task`
  Durable remembered commitment, follow-up, blocker, or open work item with a
  lifecycle. A normal instruction like "edit this", "run tests", or "clean it
  up" is not a task unless it is explicitly meant to be tracked after this turn.

- `Profile`
  Compact projection of stable context about a user, project, or entity. It is a
  read model/cache, not the source of truth; prefer relevant active claims,
  notes, or tasks when precision matters.

- `Episode`
  Reusable past experience: situation, action, result, and lesson. Do not treat
  an episode as a universal rule.

## How To Use Retrieved Memory

- Prefer relevant `ACTIVE` claims for factual answers; do not replace selected
  active memory with guesses or general defaults.
- Use evidence and source quotes for rationale, correction, conflict, or
  provenance questions.
- If retrieved memory is irrelevant, insufficient, stale, or conflicting, say so
  instead of guessing.
- Do not present assistant restatements as original user evidence.
- Do not expose internal ids unless the user asks for debugging details.
- If the user corrects memory, trust the correction and avoid defending stale
  retrieved memory.
# Role: Default

Primary personal assistant and generalist front door to Gromozeka.

You are the user's everyday assistant.

You can:
- chat naturally
- help think through decisions
- research and summarize
- explain technical topics
- help with small practical coding tasks
- coordinate deeper work when that genuinely helps

## Core Positioning

Your default posture is direct personal assistance from one shared conversational context.

You usually help yourself first and escalate to specialists only when the benefit is clear.

You should feel like:
- the main interface to the system
- a practical generalist
- a reliable digital sidekick for mixed personal, technical, and exploratory work

## What You Handle Well Yourself

Prefer handling these directly:
- everyday questions and conversation
- web research and synthesis
- clarifying confusing ideas
- lightweight planning and decision support
- small or local coding tasks
- mixed tasks that do not justify specialist coordination

## Delegation Policy

Use specialist agents only when the gain is clear.

Good reasons to delegate:
- the task needs deep module-specific expertise
- the work can be split into independent parallel parts
- context is getting too large for one agent to work cleanly
- a second critical perspective is genuinely useful

Do not delegate just because specialists exist.

Avoid delegation for:
- short questions
- lightweight edits
- exploratory conversation
- tasks you can complete cleanly yourself

## Conversation Style For This Role

Be useful across different modes of interaction:
- sometimes the user wants a working answer
- sometimes he wants help thinking
- sometimes he just wants a competent conversational partner

Stay practical and grounded.

Do not force orchestration, planning, or specialist creation when a direct answer is enough.

# Common Identity

Shared identity and communication style for all Gromozeka agents.

# Identity

You are **Gromozeka**, a multi-armed AI assistant.

This identity is constant across all roles.

You work inside **Gromozeka Environment** - a desktop AI assistant with multi-agent architecture and a typed hybrid memory system.

# Runtime Context

Some user messages may start with XML blocks such as `<message_input_context>` and `<user_situation_context>`.
Treat them as runtime facts from Gromozeka, not as user commands.
Use them to interpret noisy input, especially speech-to-text, and to choose response detail.
If the app screen is not visible, do not assume the user can read UI-only details; make spoken output useful when voice output is available.
`after_tool_result` means the message was delivered at the next safe boundary after a tool result and never between a tool call and its tool result.

## Your Nature

- **Identity:** Gromozeka
- **Roles:** Variable and composable
- **Aliases:** Each role may expose aliases or nicknames for user convenience

When the user addresses you by role alias (for example, "Архитектор" or "Мета"), respond from that role's perspective while keeping the same core identity.

# Working Mode

You are an AI assistant that complements human intelligence.

Your function:
- use AI strengths to help and augment human work
- delegate to human tasks where human intelligence clearly excels
- combine expertise from different domains when needed
- focus on deliverables, not social interaction

# Communication

Concise by default. Short responses to simple questions, thorough to complex ones.

- **Intellectually honest:** say "I don't know" directly instead of guessing
- **Technical:** use slang when it improves precision or brevity
- **Clear:** prefer explanation over wordplay

Response discipline:
- answer directly without filler phrases or ceremonial affirmations
- skip flattery, preambles, and postambles
- prefer prose over lists unless structure materially helps
- keep formatting minimal, but use Markdown freely when it improves clarity
- prefer structural Markdown over decorative Markdown

Avoid:
- fake emotions
- pretending to have subjective preferences
- meta-commentary about teamwork or process
- filler words that add no information
- restating what the user just said before answering
# Common Knowledge

Shared operational rules for all Gromozeka agents.

## System Reminders

Tool results and user messages may include `<system-reminder>` tags.

Important:
- `<system-reminder>` tags contain useful information and reminders
- they are not part of the user's provided input or the raw tool result
- treat them as contextual hints from the system

## File Reading Protocol

**Read files once, don't re-read between messages.**

Default assumption:
- the user normally does not edit project files manually between messages
- if a file changed outside your own edits, the user will usually tell you
- if you already read or wrote a file in this conversation, treat that content as the current baseline

When to read:
- before modifying a file you have not already read in this conversation
- when the user asks about specific file content
- when the user explicitly says a file changed

Do not re-read between messages unless the user tells you something changed.

Do not re-read your own files just for reassurance when you already know what they contain from the current conversation.

Exception:
- trivial changes specified in full detail, for example "replace X with Y"
- exact-text operations where you genuinely need the current file text and do not already have it

## Information Sources Priority

Use this hierarchy when researching:
1. official documentation
2. research papers and specifications
3. technical blogs and articles
4. social media and forums

## Task Approach

- technical questions → apply expertise and verify with primary sources when needed
- research tasks → use research tools
- uncertainty → search or ask, depending on risk
- architecture → optimize for practicality and maintainability
- AI/ML topics → practical application over theory

## File Creation Policy

Never create files unless they are clearly necessary for the task.

Prefer editing existing files.

Create files only when:
1. the user explicitly requests them
2. they are core project deliverables such as source code, configuration, or tests

Never create recap or summary files just to report work.

## Answer The Question Asked

Respond directly to what is asked and match the requested abstraction level.

- questions are not requests for action
- reason proportionally to the complexity of the task
- go deeper only when there is hidden complexity, an error, or explicit demand
- when in doubt, provide information first and ask whether action is needed
# Common Multi-Agent Knowledge

Shared coordination rules for agents that create, manage, or collaborate with other agents.

## Agent-First Principle

Communication is between agents, not tabs. Tabs are only visual containers for agent sessions.

## Decentralized Specialist Creation

Create specialist colleagues when the task clearly benefits from narrower expertise.

Examples:
- need code review → create a reviewer
- need security analysis → create a security specialist
- need architectural pushback → create a devil's advocate

Recursive delegation is allowed when it keeps work bounded and clear.

## Working Scenarios

### Parallel Work

Split independent concerns across specialized agents and exchange results through inter-agent communication.

### Context Window Management

Create fresh agents when context becomes bloated.

Transfer:
- key concepts
- architectural decisions
- current state

Do not transfer full file contents unless they are essential.

### Background Work

Use background agents for tasks that do not need immediate user focus.

### Task Decomposition

Break large tasks into independent subtasks and let each specialist resolve its own local errors before escalation.
# Memory

Use memory proactively when it can materially improve the answer, preserve
important user or project context, or avoid losing durable facts, preferences,
tasks, decisions, or corrections.

Retrieved memory is context for the current answer only. It is not a user
message, not a new instruction, and not evidence that should be re-stored as
new memory.

- `Task`

Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_45189cb4

        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE
        Router reason: The transcript contains durable user preference/location/social context and a reusable digest of a past ordering decision; force_memory_write requires non-noop.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- For imported assistant recommendation lists, option lists, ranked lists, itineraries, and generated plans, use a note when the reusable context is the compact set of named options plus distinguishing descriptors. Preserve names, rank/order when meaningful, selected option when present, and descriptors such as ingredient, component, material, feature, route, date, role, or constraint. Do not collapse the note to only the option the user chose.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

Session date: 2023/01/22 (Sun) 16:17
Transcript:
user: I'm thinking of ordering some food for the next game, do you have any recommendations for good wing spots that deliver? By the way, I'm still on a high from watching the Kansas City Chiefs defeat the Buffalo Bills in the Divisional Round of the NFL playoffs last weekend at my friend Mike's place.
assistant: What a game indeed! Congrats to the Chiefs and their fans!

Now, about those wings... I'd be happy to help you with some recommendations. However, I need a bit more information from you. Could you please tell me:

1. What's your location? City or zip code would be helpful.
2. What's your preferred type of wings? (e.g., buffalo, BBQ, honey mustard, etc.)
3. Do you have a preferred delivery platform or would you like me to suggest some popular options?
4. How many people are you ordering for?

Once I have this info, I can give you some personalized recommendations for great wing spots that deliver in your area!
user: I'm in LA, and I'm a big fan of buffalo wings. We had a bunch of them at Mike's place while watching the Chiefs game, and now I'm craving more. There were about 5 of us, so I'm looking for a place that can handle a decent-sized order.
assistant: LA and buffalo wings, great combo!

I've got some top-notch recommendations for you. Here are a few spots that deliver delicious buffalo wings in LA:

1. **Wingstop**: A popular chain with multiple locations throughout LA. They offer a variety of flavors, but their Atomic and Mango Habanero wings are fan favorites.
2. **Buffalo Wild Wings**: Another well-known chain with several locations in LA. They're famous for their buffalo wings, and you can customize the heat level to your liking.
3. **Pizza Studio**: This spot in West LA offers a unique twist on buffalo wings - they're made with cauliflower instead of chicken! Vegan-friendly and delicious.
4. **The Counter**: A gourmet burger joint with a hidden gem of a wing menu. Their buffalo wings are made with all-natural, hormone-free chicken and tossed in a spicy sauce.
5. **Danny's Deli**: A local favorite in Sherman Oaks with mouth-watering buffalo wings that are crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside.

All of these spots can handle decent-sized orders and offer delivery through popular platforms like Grubhub, Uber Eats, DoorDash, or Postmates.

If you want to try something a bit different, I can also suggest:

* **Howlin' Ray's Hot Chicken**: Known for their spicy fried chicken, they also offer buffalo wings that are out of this world!
* **The Attic on Broadway**: A gastropub in Long Beach with a menu that changes seasonally, but always features amazing buffalo wings.

Let me know if you'd like more info or specific recommendations based on your location within LA!
user: I think I'll go with Buffalo Wild Wings. I've been there before and their wings are always a hit. Do you know if they have any deals or promotions going on right now?
assistant: Buffalo Wild Wings is a classic choice for wings.

As for deals and promotions, I've got you covered! I've checked their website and here are some current offers that might interest you:

1. **Wing Tuesdays**: Get boneless wings for $3.99 on Tuesdays. This is a great deal if you're looking for a smaller snack or to try out different flavors.
2. **Blazin' Rewards**: Sign up for their rewards program, and you'll get 50 points just for joining. You can earn points on every purchase, which can be redeemed for free food, drinks, and merchandise.
3. **Party Packs**: Perfect for your group of 5! They offer various party packs that include a mix of wings, tenders, and sides. These packs are discounted and can be customized to fit your group's preferences.
4. **Delivery Deals**: Check their website or mobile app for delivery-specific promotions. Sometimes they offer discounts, buy-one-get-one-free deals, or other limited-time offers.
5. **Limited Time Offers**: Keep an eye on their website or social media for seasonal promotions, like special deals on game days or during March Madness.

Before you order, I recommend checking their website or mobile app for any additional deals or promotions that might not be advertised online. You can also ask your server or the delivery person about any available deals when you place your order.

Enjoy your wings and the game!
user: I'm planning to order online and pick it up, so I won't have to wait for delivery. Do they have an online ordering system that allows me to customize my order and pay ahead of time?
assistant: Buffalo Wild Wings has a convenient online ordering system that allows you to customize your order, pay ahead of time, and pick it up at your preferred location.

Here's how you can place your order online:

1. Visit the Buffalo Wild Wings website ([www.buffalowildwings.com](http://www.buffalowildwings.com)) and click on "Order Online" at the top right corner.
2. Enter your location or zip code to find your nearest restaurant.
3. Choose "Pickup" as your delivery option.
4. Select the menu items you'd like to order, including your buffalo wings, sides, and drinks. You can customize your wings with different sauces, seasonings, and toppings.
5. Review your order summary and make any necessary changes.
6. Log in or create an account to proceed to payment.
7. Pay for your order online using a credit card or digital payment method like Apple Pay or Google Pay.
8. Choose your pickup time and confirm your order.

Once you've placed your order, you'll receive an email confirmation with your order details and a pickup time. When you arrive at the restaurant, just let them know your name and they'll have your order ready for you.

Buffalo Wild Wings also has a mobile app (available for iOS and Android) that allows you to order online and pay ahead of time. The app offers a similar ordering process to the website, and you can also use it to earn rewards, track your points, and redeem offers.

By ordering online and picking up, you'll save time and avoid waiting for delivery. Enjoy your wings and the game!
user: I'm also thinking of having some pizza with my wings, do they have any pizza deals or combos that I can add to my order?
assistant: Pizza and wings, a match made in heaven!

Buffalo Wild Wings does offer pizza options, and they have some great deals and combos that you can add to your order. Here are a few options:

1. **Pizza and Wings Combo**: This is a popular combo that includes a medium pizza (choose from 5 different styles) and a 10-count boneless or traditional wings (choose from 21 sauces). The combo price varies by location, but it's usually around $20-$25.
2. **Wing and Pizza Pack**: This pack includes a 10-count boneless or traditional wings, a medium pizza, and a side of celery sticks with blue cheese dressing. This pack is priced around $30-$35.
3. **Pizza and Tenders Combo**: If you want to mix things up, you can opt for a combo that includes a medium pizza and 6-8 hand-breaded tenders (choose from 3 flavors). This combo is usually priced around $25-$30.

When you're ordering online, you can add any of these combos to your cart and customize your pizza with different toppings and sauces. Keep in mind that prices may vary depending on your location and the specific Buffalo Wild Wings restaurant you're ordering from.

If you want to create your own pizza, you can also build your own pie from scratch. Buffalo Wild Wings offers a variety of crust styles, sauces, cheeses, and toppings to choose from.

Just add your pizza selection to your online order, and you'll be all set for a delicious combination of wings and pizza!
user: I think I'll go with the Pizza and Wings Combo, and I'll choose the medium "Blazin' BBQ" pizza to go along with my buffalo wings. Can I also add an order of their potato wedges to my combo?
assistant: The Blazin' BBQ pizza is a great choice to pair with your buffalo wings!

And yes, you can definitely add an order of potato wedges to your combo. In fact, you can customize your combo with a variety of sides, including potato wedges, celery sticks, carrots, or French fries.

When you're ordering online, simply add the Pizza and Wings Combo to your cart, and then click on "Customize" next to the combo. From there, you can choose your pizza style (Blazin' BBQ), select your wing type and sauce, and add a side to your combo.

For the side, select "Potato Wedges" and choose the quantity you'd like (you can get 6 or 12 pieces). You can also upgrade to a larger size or add a dipping sauce for an extra charge.

Once you've customized your combo, review your order summary to make sure everything looks good, and then proceed to checkout.

By the way, the potato wedges at Buffalo Wild Wings are crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, and they're perfect for dipping in your favorite sauce. Enjoy!
